title: McGraw-Hill Data Structures Learning Path
description: A visual map showing the progression of topics in a typical McGraw-Hill authored 'Introduction to Data Structures' textbook, starting from foundational concepts and branching into specific data structure types.
elements: Linear Structures (Arrays, Linked Lists, Stacks, Queues)
relationships: Foundational concepts lead to understanding of memory, which is required to learn both Linear and Non-Linear structures. Algorithms are applied across all structure types to manipulate data.
